description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly reddish-brown coloration, interspersed with lighter beige and tan patches. The surface is uneven, with a mix of rounded and slightly angular contours, and displays natural weathering marks and subtle textural variations. The rock appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings, and its surface finish is mostly matte with some areas showing a faint, natural sheen. Its form suggests it could serve as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a park or roadside. The coloration and texture indicate it is likely composed of sedimentary stone, possibly sandstone or a similar material. There are no visible signs of artificial modification, markings, or integration with other objects.
description: A solid, irregular reddish-brown rock with lighter beige patches and a rough, weathered surface. Its dense, natural form makes it suitable as an urban barrier or landscape feature.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the rock, highlighting its reddish-brown color with beige streaks and a gently undulating surface.
description_view_1: From this angle, the rock appears more compact, with a rounded top and a slightly sloped side, emphasizing its mass and solid form.
description_view_2: This side profile reveals the elongated dimension of the rock, with a smoother, more continuous surface and subtle color gradations.
description_view_3: The end view displays a more vertical face, with visible indentations and a mix of reddish and beige tones, giving a sense of its thickness.
